what_it_does: A self-contained Workshop editor for ConceptV widgets. Defines widget kinds (dashboard tile / virtual-hub / embeddable) and systems (kpi / media / hybrid) with mock feeds and sparkline datasets; an AI drafter (viDraftWidget) that prompts the model to produce widget JSON from a brief; a presentational WidgetRender that renders the widget body through the shared core engine; the WidgetBuilder editor (kind/system pickers, data panel, live stage, variations, candidate gallery, export); a WidgetStage with simulated live-data rotation and skeleton-resolve; context frames showing the widget inside dashboard/hub/embed mockups; and a JSX/PNG/PDF export menu.
observations: WidgetRender renders the body via window.__cvContainmentTree / __cvDiagramSolver / __cvTypeToNode and throws loudly if the core engine isn't loaded ('unification requires window.__cvContainmentTree')
viDraftWidget injects window.CV_AI.get('voice.conceptv').text into the prompt and calls window.CV_AI.complete, then parses JSON with a brace-regex fallback, throwing if unparsable
Authorship is made legible via a 'by by-vi'/'by by-human' mark derived from doc.author or doc.mocked, citing DESIGN-LANGUAGE §11
WidgetStage simulates a live feed: rotates trend sparkdata and deterministically nudges KPI numeric values on a 2200ms tick, plus a 620ms skeleton-resolve on mock toggle (cites states.css §10)
Candidate gallery flow uses window.WS_AI.generateCandidates / applyDiff and a window.WSCandidateGallery component
Pickers read window.WIDGET_KINDS/WIDGET_SYSTEMS if present (falling back to the module constants), and show a provenance badge for non-built-in entries
Export menu builds standalone JSX via widgetToJSX, a foreignObject-SVG PNG export with cross-origin caveat, and window.print PDF
Promote-to-Type via window.CV_TYPES_SAVE.fromWidget + window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT.open; save-as-template via onSaveTemplate
co-brand path uses two hardcoded hex colors (#2A78C6 partner blue, #DEEAF7 soft) instead of tokens
standouts: The widget body is rendered THROUGH the one shared engine (T2N → ContainmentTree) rather than bespoke widget markup, with a loud throw if the engine is absent — an explicit unification seam
Simulated 'live' data: deterministic KPI drift + trend rotation + skeleton-resolve to make a mock feel alive
Provenance/authorship vocabulary (.by-vi / .by-human) reused from the engine's zoning rather than parallel chrome
conventions: Follows the project's window-global registry pattern (CV_AI.complete, voice.conceptv sourced not inlined, core engine resolved off window) and exports components onto window
Uses CSS var() tokens extensively for the card chrome
Breaks the no-raw-px/hex rule: the export-JSX template and many inline styles hardcode px values, and the co-brand colors are raw hex (#2A78C6 / #DEEAF7)
concerns: Co-brand colors are hardcoded hex (#2A78C6, #DEEAF7) — a second home for color values, against the project's single-source token rule
The exported standalone JSX hardcodes pixel literals and reimplements the KPI/CTA layout (a parallel rendering path divergent from the engine-rendered preview)
PNG export via foreignObject is acknowledged as possibly non-working cross-browser (htmlToInline is a minimal clone)
notes: The full Workshop widget editor — kind/system model + mock feeds + a Vi drafter + an engine-rendered preview with simulated live data + variations + export; note it renders through the shared core engine and throws if that engine is missing.
questions: What is the exact shape returned by WS_AI.generateCandidates and the diff format applyDiff consumes?
Does T2N with family:'widget'/kind:'widget-system' have a registered renderer for all three systems?
Why does the export path reimplement layout instead of serializing the engine output?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header says a self-contained editor for KPI/media/hybrid widgets across dashboard/hub/embed contexts, and the code delivers exactly that plus AI drafting and export.
apparent_themes: Unification — one engine renders every surface (widgets included), no parallel parts
AI-as-collaborator (Vi drafts, refines, generates candidate alternatives) with the shared voice
Context-in-situ previewing (widget shown inside its real host frame)
intention_signals: Aims to let a designer build a ConceptV widget once and see it rendered by the same engine that drives every other surface, inside its real deployment context, with Vi able to draft/refine/offer alternatives, and then export it as code or image — all single-sourced from the registries and token system.
novelty: Rendering a 'widget' through the generic Type→Node→ContainmentTree engine (treating a widget as just another system family) and the deterministic 'fake live feed' that nudges KPI numbers and rotates sparklines to simulate real data.
contribution: Contributes a complete widget-authoring canvas: a widget kind/system taxonomy with size specs, a Vi widget-drafting prompt+parser, an engine-rendered in-context preview with simulated live data and skeleton-resolve, a system/kind variations panel, and a multi-format (JSX/PNG/PDF) export.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV Workshop widget builder defining dashboard/hub/embed kinds and kpi/media/hybrid systems, with an AI drafter, an engine-rendered in-context preview with simulated live data, variations, candidate gallery, and JSX/PNG/PDF export.
intention_for_embedding: To author widgets once and render them through the one shared engine inside their real host context, with Vi drafting/refining/offering alternatives and code/image export, all single-sourced from the token and AI registries.
